text:
Dev Patel: Hey @Sean Gallagher @Rafael Mendes — can you take a quick look at the proposed Support SLA table before we publish? Main question: does this align w/ paging thresholds + error budget policy. Draft: https://confluence.redwood.ai/x/support-sla-policy-draft

Dev Patel: TL;DR targets in draft:
- Enterprise P0: first response 15m, updates every 30m
- Enterprise P1: first response 1h, updates every 2h
- Dedicated P0: first response 30m, updates every 1h
- Hosted self-serve: best-effort within business hours

Sean Gallagher: Looking now. First reaction: the “updates every 30m” language for Enterprise P0 is fine for *comms*, but the escalation trigger column doesn’t match how we actually page / declare incidents.

Sean Gallagher: Example mismatch: draft says “page SRE for P0 if >5% error rate for 10m OR >3 customers impacted”. Our SLO alerting/paging policy is still “global availab...

Allison Grant: also seeing increased DB connections in pg dashboard. feels like query regression rather than infra.

text:
Aisha Bello: pulling out action items from today’s perf-canary retro so we don’t lose them. pls reply in-thread if you’re an owner / can’t take it.

AIs (draft):
1) Tuning: min-samples + dampening for warning + page (reduce flaps) — owner: @Rafael Mendes
2) Runbook updates: add clearer “is it queueing vs runtime exec” decision tree + rollback triggers — owner: @Aisha Bello (with @Noah Patel for runtime bits)
3) Alert payload: fix dashboard links (region/model preselected) + add trace link + baseline artifact link — owner: @Allison Grant
4) Baseline drift handling: document/update policy + guardrails to avoid “baseline chasing prod noise” — owner: @Nadia Rahman
5) Cardinality follow-up: confirm label set + retention impact after adding queue/batch histos — owner: @Logan Wright
6) Paging hygiene: top-model page rules need explicit “sustained” definition + dedupe key in incident-bot — ow...

text:
Aisha Bello: seeing a bunch of noise from the new auto-rollback alert rules in staging + a couple prod canaries. mostly `rollout_auto_action_triggered` and `rollout_slo_monitor_stale` flipping during the telemetry hiccup in eu-west. can we tune before we light up more services?

GrafanaBot: Alert triggered: rollout_slo_monitor_stale
Labels: service=rollout-controller region=eu-west tier=internal severity=page
Annotations: "SLO burn-rate endpoint returned stale data (age=198s) for 3/5 consecutive polls"

Sean Gallagher: yeah, paging for stale monitors during known telemetry issues is not what we want. we should be notifying (slack) not paging, and ideally suppress entirely when telemetry is degraded.

Rafael Mendes: +1. last night we had the metrics-ingest backlog, and everything lit up. oncall got 12 pages in 8 min. :/

Logan Wright: telemetry side: confirmed we had query lag from the...

title: #eng-sre: 1770932256
preview:
Sean Gallagher: We need to settle operational ownership for auto-rollback events. Who gets paged when an auto-rollback triggers? SRE vs service owner? Also how do we treat multi-tenant impact (Hosted pool vs Dedicated vs Private).

Aisha Bello: +1. I’m wiring alert rules now and need routing. Right now I have “auto_...
text:
Sean Gallagher: We need to settle operational ownership for auto-rollback events. Who gets paged when an auto-rollback triggers? SRE vs service owner? Also how do we treat multi-tenant impact (Hosted pool vs Dedicated vs Private).

Aisha Bello: +1. I’m wiring alert rules now and need routing. Right now I have “auto_rollback_triggered” as notify-only in #eng-sre but not paging. Unsure if that’s enough.

Rafael Mendes: From oncall perspective: if rollback succeeded, it *already mitigated* the customer-facing issue. Paging on every trigger will be noisy.

Jada Williams: Agree. Controller will emit decision = pause/rollback/suppress. I’d propose paging only on “rollback_failed” or “monitor_missing/stale” or “suppressed_due_to_incident=false but still breached” type states.

Allison Grant: Also: multi-tenant vs single-tenant. A rollback on Hosted might indicate a systemic regression in sha...

text:
Aisha Bello: Heads up: doing a short oncall training session for perf-canary alerts (warn/page). Target audience: SRE oncall + runtime secondary. 

Proposed time: Thu 11:00–11:45 PT. Agenda:
1) What the canary is / what it is NOT
2) How to validate a warning vs a page
3) Quick attribution: queueing vs runtime vs fleet
4) Mitigation + rollback + disable switch
5) Q&A

Pls reply with :thumbsup: if you can make it, and any questions you want covered.

Sean Gallagher: :thumbsup: works. Can we make sure we cover “what do I do if dashboards are behind” / telemetry lag? We hit that last week.

Rafael Mendes: :thumbsup: +1. Also want clarity on paging hygiene: when do we wake runtime vs handle as SRE with a mitigation.

Allison Grant: :thumbsup: I can join. Suggest we also explicitly cover dedupe/anti-flap so folks don’t panic when they see multiple alerts for same model.

Noah Patel: :thumbs...

preview:

text:
Aisha Bello: pulling folks in here re: dashboards + alert routing for auto-rollback. need to decide what *pages* vs what’s slack-only, and how we tag rollout ids so oncall can investigate quickly (without blowing up cardinality). anyone have strong opinions before I update alert rules in observability-pack?

Sean Gallagher: yup. biggest thing: we can’t page every time a rollback triggers, otherwise we’ll get paged during normal safe operation. paging should be for “safety rail failed” or “high-impact condition”.

Allison Grant: +1. also rollout_id as a metric label is going to be a no from standards. it’ll explode. should be annotation / dashboard variable / log correlation.

Rafael Mendes: agreed. from oncall perspective, I want:
- a single dashboard where I can answer “what rollout changed?”
- one link in the alert that takes me to rollout timeline + decision explanation
- and a cle...

title: #eng-sre: 1770468009
preview:
Aisha Bello: Question for perf-canary alerting: do we want thresholds to be region/SKU-specific? Seeing eu-west p95 variance higher than us-east on same model, and H100 vs A100 behaves differently on tokens/sec.

Allison Grant: +1 we need to be careful. If we make alert rules key off region+sku+model+version we’re g...
text:
Aisha Bello: Question for perf-canary alerting: do we want thresholds to be region/SKU-specific? Seeing eu-west p95 variance higher than us-east on same model, and H100 vs A100 behaves differently on tokens/sec.

Allison Grant: +1 we need to be careful. If we make alert rules key off region+sku+model+version we’re gonna explode config + paging complexity.

Sean Gallagher: The oncall pain is mostly: page fires in 1 region and people ask “is this real or just eu-west being eu-west”. If we keep global thresholds, we’ll either over-page in noisier regions or miss regressions in stable ones.

Rafael Mendes: What’s the actual delta we’re seeing? If it’s like +10-15% baseline variance in eu-west, we can bake it into warning thresholds. But for paging, I’d rather not have region-specific behavior unless there’s a strong reason.

Nadia Rahman: From baseline-results-week1, eu-west A100 pool has...

text:
PerfCanary: :rotating_light: perf-canary failure: `billing_meter_invariants` (run_id=pc-2026-02-24-0812)
Scenario: `streaming_retry_fallback_metering`
Result: FAILED (invariant violation: duplicate billed tokens)

Failure summary:
- expected `billed_completion_tokens` to be monotonic + equal to final completion length
- observed `billed_completion_tokens` increased twice for same `request_id` during server-side retry

Links:
- perf-canary PR (scenario): https://github.com/perf-canary/pr-912-add-billing-meter-regression-scenarios
- last green run: pc-2026-02-24-0640
- last deploy to canary: redwood@9c1f3a1 (includes PR-27555)

Log excerpt:
```

chunk=7 usage_delta: +completion_tokens=128 +billed_completion_tokens=128
server_retry: upstream_timeout -> fallback_model=rw-llama-70b-instruct
chunk=1 usage_delta: +completion_tokens=64 +bil...

title: #eng-sre: 1770183307
preview:
Connor O'Brien: heads up, need SRE eyes on proposed backfill plan + risk before we push the button.

Context: we have ~3.1% of Hosted API usage_events missing `cache_hit` + ~0.6% missing `region` over the last ~45 days (mostly from the ingestion retry window + one bad deploy). Product/Finance want the backfill so us...
text:
Connor O'Brien: heads up, need SRE eyes on proposed backfill plan + risk before we push the button.

Context: we have ~3.1% of Hosted API usage_events missing `cache_hit` + ~0.6% missing `region` over the last ~45 days (mostly from the ingestion retry window + one bad deploy). Product/Finance want the backfill so usage exports + experiment attribution stop being noisy.

Proposal (current):
- Run a replay job over `request_logs` -> regenerate usage events with the v2 mapper (same codepath as live)
- Write into `usage_events_v2_backfill` (partitioned by `event_day`)
- Dedupe using `usage_event_dedupe_key` (request_id + attempt_id + response_fingerprint)
- After backfill completes, flip the `usage_events_current` view to union (live v2 + backfill for historical partitions)

Ask: do we need to run shadow billing compare first? and what are the biggest "oops we changed billing numbers" ris...

title: #eng-sre: 1770119025
preview:
Aisha Bello: need to lock down *freshness SLOs* for billing tables before we turn on paging. right now we have “it seems late” :grimacing:

Aisha Bello: context: Finance wants predictable lag for month-end + we need clear paging conditions + owners. can folks sanity check targets?

Mei Lin: +1. also need to be expli...
text:
Aisha Bello: need to lock down *freshness SLOs* for billing tables before we turn on paging. right now we have “it seems late” :grimacing:

Aisha Bello: context: Finance wants predictable lag for month-end + we need clear paging conditions + owners. can folks sanity check targets?

Mei Lin: +1. also need to be explicit what we measure: partition watermark vs max(event_time) is tricky w/ late arrivals.

Caleb Johnson: agreed. for pipelines we usually use `ingest_watermark_ts` (time the partition landed) not `event_time`.

Logan Wright: +1. event_time can be arbitrarily late w/ retries/backfills. SLO should be about “data available for billing aggregation” i.e. `pipeline_processed_ts`.

Emily Stone: Finance perspective: we don’t need real-time, but we do need *stable* daily totals for invoice preview + close. if it’s late, we need to know early + have an escalation path.

Aisha Bello: c...

text:
Aisha Bello: heads up, meter drift alerts are paging *way* too often since we turned on the new by-account/model/region breakdown. I got 6 pages overnight, all < $20 impact and mostly late-event catchup. Can we tune thresholds today?

Grafana: Alert firing: `meter_drift_vs_ledger` (severity=page)

Value: drift_pct=1.12%, drift_tokens=18,440

Logan Wright: +1, also saw it. But I don’t want to over-tune and miss real issues like the retry double-counting bug (ENG-4430) we just fixed.

Mei Lin: I pulled a quick sample from last night’s pages. 4/6 were caused by usage_events arriving ~90min late (warehouse lag), then the drift “self-healed” on next eval.

Caleb Johnson: yep ingestion was behind. snowpipe backlog after a schema deploy. caught up by ~03:10 P...

title: #eng-sre: 1770039055
preview:
Sean Gallagher: Trying to unblock defaults for auto-rollback MVP. We need sane thresholds + windows that won’t flap us into constant pause/rollback. Can folks weigh in on burn-rate patterns we’ve used successfully? Specifically p95/p99 latency + 5xx error rate.

Aisha Bello: +1. From alerting side we can support mul...
text:
Sean Gallagher: Trying to unblock defaults for auto-rollback MVP. We need sane thresholds + windows that won’t flap us into constant pause/rollback. Can folks weigh in on burn-rate patterns we’ve used successfully? Specifically p95/p99 latency + 5xx error rate.

Aisha Bello: +1. From alerting side we can support multi-window burn rate like (5m, 30m) fast/slow, and optionally (1h, 6h) for longer SLOs. But the controller needs a deterministic rule set (and min sample size) or we’ll get rollback storms.

Rafael Mendes: Historical data point: the “reranker v3” incident last fall. Traffic ramped from 5% to 25%, p99 popped for ~7 minutes (GC + cache warm), then settled. If we had a naive “p99 > threshold for 5m => rollback” it would have rolled back unnecessarily.

Rafael Mendes: Another: dedicated customer in us-east, transient 502 burst due to upstream auth. Would have triggered error-rat...

title: #eng-sre: 1769792803
preview:
Aisha Bello: Dropping feedback on the new SLO toolkit templates (perf canary use case). We’re trying to represent “per-model perf regression” as an SLO-like signal (warn/page + burn-ish behavior), but the existing templates assume classic request success/failure.

Aisha Bello: Specifically: we want to alert when p95...
text:
Aisha Bello: Dropping feedback on the new SLO toolkit templates (perf canary use case). We’re trying to represent “per-model perf regression” as an SLO-like signal (warn/page + burn-ish behavior), but the existing templates assume classic request success/failure.

Aisha Bello: Specifically: we want to alert when p95 latency regresses vs baseline by >X% for model=M in region=R (optionally sku), w/ min sample size + dampening. How should this be modeled in slo-toolkit so it’s not a one-off alert rule?

Allison Grant: +1. The toolkit’s “availability” template is basically error_ratio over requests. For perf regressions we don’t really have an “event” unless we define one.

Sean Gallagher: From oncall POV: I want something that behaves like SLOs (multiwindow, anti-flap, paging hygiene) but I do NOT want people interpreting it as “customer SLO is violated” every time canary is noisy.

Rafa...

preview:

Allison Grant: +1. also we should decide what we ...
text:
Aisha Bello: for the runtime 1.21 canary (kernel auto-selection v2), can we align on rollout monitoring? specifically: which dashboards/alerts are “source of truth”, what sampling rate for selector telemetry, and what triggers a rollback vs “watch + tweak denylist”.

Allison Grant: +1. also we should decide what we trust for p99. the new kernel-selection dashboard panels are great but they’re new queries + new labels.

Sean Gallagher: current stance from SRE: the existing SLO dashboards (api latency + inference tail) stay the primary rollback signal. kernel-selection dashboard is diagnostic.

Omar El-Sayed: agree. kernel-selection panels are helpful to attribute (selection distribution shifts, chosen kernel), but they’re not the first tripwire.

Aisha Bello: ok so rollback triggers based on existing: p95/p99 + error rate. then use kernel-selection dashboards to figure out why.

text:
Rafael Mendes: Paging hygiene review (week 1) — can we do a quick async sweep of what paged/noised and what we want to tune before we expand to eu-west?

Rafael Mendes: Data points from oncall notes:
- 6 pages total from perf-canary paging rules
- 2 were real-ish (queueing time spike tied to fleet move)
- 4 were either baseline mismatch or dedupe/threading issues
- warning channel got spammy around Tue/Thu (lots of near-threshold flaps)

Rafael Mendes: Biggest pain: incident-bot created multiple threads for what looked like the same regression (same model + region) and then another page fired 15m later even though we were already in the thread. :facepalm:

Aisha Bello: +1. I pulled the timeline from Grafana + PagerDuty.

Aisha Bello: Patterns:
1) “page -> clears -> page again” within 20–40m (classic flap)
2) Multiple distinct pages for the same model but different label sets (tier/hw_...

title: #eng-sre: 1768821107
preview:
Aisha Bello: we should draft the statuspage comms playbook now, before we flip more traffic to tiered batching. what are the criteria for posting if latency worsens during rollout?

Rafael Mendes: +1. we’ve got abort criteria for canary, but statuspage criteria is separate (customer-visible). don’t want to over-post...
text:
Aisha Bello: we should draft the statuspage comms playbook now, before we flip more traffic to tiered batching. what are the criteria for posting if latency worsens during rollout?

Rafael Mendes: +1. we’ve got abort criteria for canary, but statuspage criteria is separate (customer-visible). don’t want to over-post for a 1% canary blip.

Sean Gallagher: agreed. let’s define:
1) when we post
2) what we say (don’t overspec internal feature names)
3) who owns updates + cadence

Allison Grant: from metrics side, suggest we anchor on customer-visible SLOs and impact scope.
- Hosted API: fast tier p99 (streaming first-token) + standard tier p95 end-to-end are the ones folks feel
- also “time to first token” for streaming routes is super sensitive

Marissa Cole: also need a line on “if this is limited to canary cohort” -> we can do targeted comms (support + affected customers) instead of gl...

title: #eng-sre: 1768793301
preview: Rafael Mendes: hey folks — need an SLO proposal for the Cost Model API before we bless Console hot-path integration. what are we thinking for availability/latency + how we define the drift/correctness SLI? also flagging alerting risks: telemetry lag + changing schema semantics can make “drift” look like a production...
text:
Rafael Mendes: hey folks — need an SLO proposal for the Cost Model API before we bless Console hot-path integration. what are we thinking for availability/latency + how we define the drift/correctness SLI? also flagging alerting risks: telemetry lag + changing schema semantics can make “drift” look like a production regression.

Ethan Park: +1, we’ve got a draft SLO doc started but it’s not reviewed. we were targeting internal tier 0.5-ish (Console depends on it but we’ll have fallback). for latency we were thinking p95 < 150ms for `estimate` and p95 < 300ms for `breakdown` (bounded responses only).

Allison Grant: before numbers: what’s the dependency graph + what’s “critical”? if Console is calling synchronously, SLO needs to reflect that OR we mandate caching/fallback and treat Cost Model API as tier-1 dependency but not page-on-everything.

Talia Benson: Console integration is beh...

title: #eng-sre: 1768680002
preview:
Rafael Mendes: hey folks — need quick eyes on the customer comms draft for the planned regional failover test next week. want to lock wording + timing + escalation contacts today so Support isn’t improvising.

Rafael Mendes: draft is here (Google doc): https://drive.google.com/redwood/failover/customer-comms-draft-p...
text:
Rafael Mendes: hey folks — need quick eyes on the customer comms draft for the planned regional failover test next week. want to lock wording + timing + escalation contacts today so Support isn’t improvising.

Rafael Mendes: draft is here (Google doc): https://drive.google.com/redwood/failover/customer-comms-draft-planned-failover

Kira Thompson: skimming now. first reaction: we say “no customer impact expected” in the first paragraph. i’d soften that.

Kira Thompson: maybe “we do not expect significant impact, but you may see brief increases in latency or transient errors during the window.”

Dev Patel: +1. Support gets burned when we use absolutes.

Owen Phillips: agree. can we also define what “brief” means? like “<2 minutes” if that’s true, or just “short-lived”.

Rafael Mendes: good call. we *hope* <60s but don’t want to promise. i can say “short-lived” and keep the quantitative ...

title: #eng-sre: 1768671100
preview:
Miles Thornton: need to settle this: do we require manual approval hooks for prod failover controller, or go fully auto once signals trip?

Rafael Mendes: for initial rollout i strongly prefer approval in prod. we *just* got past the noisy signal issue in staging. last thing we need is a thundering herd because cont...
text:
Miles Thornton: need to settle this: do we require manual approval hooks for prod failover controller, or go fully auto once signals trip?

Rafael Mendes: for initial rollout i strongly prefer approval in prod. we *just* got past the noisy signal issue in staging. last thing we need is a thundering herd because controller flipped twice.

Benji Okafor: +1 to approval as default, but i’d like us to be specific about what “approval” means. on-call click button? slack /ack? timeboxed hold? otherwise it becomes theater.

Mina Haddad: from routing side: full automation is the end state. manual approval doesn’t scale and we’ll miss SLOs if we wait for a human at 3am. but i’m ok with phased.

Allison Grant: FYI dashboards/alerts can support either. bigger issue is: can on-call reliably tell it’s a real region impairment vs a localized 5xx spike? if not, approval just delays and still fails.

...

title: #eng-sre: 1768634300
preview:

text:
Rafael Mendes: heads up — we got paged 6 times overnight on the new multi-region burn-rate rules. mostly recovered by the time someone opened the dash. feels noisy.

PagerDuty: Triggered: [P1] multi_region_availability_burn_rate_fast (service=hosted-api, region=eu-west) — https://pagerduty.redwood.ai/incidents/PQ7K2

Tomáš Novák: yeah, that was me at 02:14. opened the failover dash and it looked green-ish except a small blip on `/v1/chat.completions`. still paged.

Allison Grant: can someone paste the alert annotation from the firing? want to see which clause tripped (fast vs slow window).

Sergio Costa: from Alertmanager:
```
alertname=multi_region_availability_burn_rate_fast
scope=hosted
route_group=default
region=eu-west
window=5m
burnrate=17.8
threshold=14
```
Also slow window was *not* firing.

Miles Thornton: 17.8x over 5m with slow not corroborating sounds like either a brief 5...

title: #eng-sre: 1768526344
preview:
Rafael Mendes: We need to settle anti-flap + dedupe for perf-canary alerts before we turn on paging. Current incident-bot handler is basically “one Slack thread per firing alertname”, which is… not enough.

Allison Grant: +1. We’re going to get spam if p95 oscillates around threshold. Need: (1) alert rule dampening,...
text:
Rafael Mendes: We need to settle anti-flap + dedupe for perf-canary alerts before we turn on paging. Current incident-bot handler is basically “one Slack thread per firing alertname”, which is… not enough.

Allison Grant: +1. We’re going to get spam if p95 oscillates around threshold. Need: (1) alert rule dampening, (2) incident-bot dedupe key, (3) clear “resolved” behavior.

Aisha Bello: For alerting side: what are we thinking for minimum samples? If the canary is low QPS for overhead reasons, we can’t do 1-min windows.

Nadia Rahman: FWIW telemetry pipeline produces stable histograms at 1m resolution but variance is high under 50 req/min, especially for p95/p99. I’d strongly prefer the evaluator enforce a min sample size gate.

Sean Gallagher: From on-call perspective: fewer pages > faster pages. I’m ok delaying page by 10–15m if it reduces false positives.

Logan Wright: Dedupe key...

title: #eng-sre: 1768479010
preview:
Rafael Mendes: need to lock acceptance criteria for failover controller dry-run this week. what *exactly* must it output so we can call ENG-2415 “done”? drop bullets here.

Miles Thornton: +1. baseline: dry-run should behave like prod decisioning but with zero writes. Output needs to be enough for on-call to say “ye...
text:
Rafael Mendes: need to lock acceptance criteria for failover controller dry-run this week. what *exactly* must it output so we can call ENG-2415 “done”? drop bullets here.

Miles Thornton: +1. baseline: dry-run should behave like prod decisioning but with zero writes. Output needs to be enough for on-call to say “yes execute” or “nope false positive”.

Jordan Lee: from policy engine side: MUST be deterministic given the same signal snapshot + config (no nondeterministic ordering). also include the “decision trace” so we can reproduce.

Mina Haddad: pls include policy diff output. like “route chat/completions tier=hosted: us-east weight 100→0, eu-west 0→100” and any fallbacks invoked (variant/model) if applicable.

Benji Okafor: and scope. “applies_to”: tenant tier / allowlist / max blast radius gates that would be enforced if executed. dry-run should show what it *would* block too.

A...

title: #eng-sre: 1768476201
preview:
Rafael Mendes: heading into prod game day next week — can we align on *must-have* dashboards + alert tuning? I don’t want us staring at 12 tabs and missing the one signal that matters. What’s the minimum set + what annotations do we need in place before go/no-go?

Allison Grant: +1. For game day we need a single “Fa...
text:
Rafael Mendes: heading into prod game day next week — can we align on *must-have* dashboards + alert tuning? I don’t want us staring at 12 tabs and missing the one signal that matters. What’s the minimum set + what annotations do we need in place before go/no-go?

Allison Grant: +1. For game day we need a single “Failover Game Day” folder (Grafana) with panels in the order we’ll use them.
Must-have (IMO):
1) Region health aggregate (overall + per-signal)
2) Cross-region reachability probes (perf-canary) w/ p50/p95 + failure rate
3) API 5xx burn rate (multiwindow) split by region + route group
4) Tail latency burn rate (p99) split by region + route group
5) Capacity headroom + queue depth per region (hosted + dedicated)
6) Policy engine decisions: recommended vs executed flips, reason + confidence
7) Gateway retry/timeout counters (to catch thundering herd)
8) Dedicated drain progress ...

text:
Allison Grant: seeing some noisy trigger signals in staging today. region-health is flipping `us-east` between degraded/ok every few mins, and failover-controller dry-run is recommending failover ~1x/10m 😬

Allison Grant: dashboard link (staging): https://grafana.staging.redwood/p/d/failover-signals?var=region=us-east

Victor Alvarez: +1 I saw a few probe timeouts. might be our NAT gateway in staging, not the region itself. the TCP handshake is fine but the HTTP probe sometimes stalls.

Sergio Costa: if reachability is treated as authoritative (ADR-042 direction), this will create false positives unless we dampen / corroborate.

text:
Talia Benson: Kicking off plan review for Q1 controlled failover game day (us-east -> eu-west). Need owners + go/no-go by EOD tomorrow so we can send exec update. Draft plan is in Confluence: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/SRE/Controlled+Failover+Test+Plan+Q1+2026

Rafael Mendes: +1. Also want a very crisp checklist for go/no-go. We got burned last rehearsal by “we assumed X was on”.

Miles Thornton: I skimmed the plan. Overall looks good. A few big questions:
1) What’s our authoritative trigger for declaring “region impaired” during the test (since we’re inducing it)?

title: #eng-sre: 1768438820
preview:
Aisha Bello: Kicking off severity policy for perf-canary alerts. Need agreement on: what’s WARN vs PAGE, and who gets paged when a top-model regresses. Goal is to avoid paging on noise but still catch real tail latency regressions fast.

Aisha Bello: Proposal v0:
- WARN: p95 e2e latency +8% vs baseline sustained 10m...
text:
Aisha Bello: Kicking off severity policy for perf-canary alerts. Need agreement on: what’s WARN vs PAGE, and who gets paged when a top-model regresses. Goal is to avoid paging on noise but still catch real tail latency regressions fast.

Aisha Bello: Proposal v0:
- WARN: p95 e2e latency +8% vs baseline sustained 10m (min N=200) OR tokens/sec -8% sustained 10m (min N=200)
- PAGE (Sev2): p95 +15% sustained 15m (min N=500) OR tokens/sec -15% sustained 15m (min N=500)
- PAGE (Sev1): p95 +25% sustained 15m (min N=500) AND affects >=2 regions OR top-model in us-east

Aisha Bello: Routing:
- WARN -> #eng-runtime + #eng-sre (no page)
- PAGE -> oncall (SRE primary) + runtime secondary in thread

Sean Gallagher: +1 on tiered, but I’d like paging to be *only* for top-models initially. For non-top models, page is going to kill us during tuning.

Rafael Mendes: Agree. If we page for any model regr...

title: #eng-sre: 1768148800
preview:
Sean Gallagher: Want to close the loop on “fail-open” + circuit breaker behavior for shadow traffic before we finalize alerts/runbook. I’m seeing slightly different assumptions between routing + telemetry + scoring.

Sean Gallagher: Requirements from SRE POV:
1) shadow path *cannot* add tail latency to user response...
text:
Sean Gallagher: Want to close the loop on “fail-open” + circuit breaker behavior for shadow traffic before we finalize alerts/runbook. I’m seeing slightly different assumptions between routing + telemetry + scoring.

Sean Gallagher: Requirements from SRE POV:
1) shadow path *cannot* add tail latency to user response (beyond noise)
2) shadow path failures must not reduce control path success rate
3) when shadow target or scoring/ingestion is unhealthy, we degrade by dropping shadow work (not retrying inline)

Mina Haddad: +1. On routing side we can make mirror fully async / detached from request lifecycle. For streaming we already decided: never wait on shadow chunks.

Ravi Menon: Same. Data plane will do “best effort enqueue” and then move on. But we need explicit timeout + breaker semantics so we’re not doing expensive work when the target is on fire.

Allison Grant: Observability an...

text:
Allison Grant: jumping in w/ quick cardinality risk review for shadow traffic dims before we bless ENG-5410 + dashboards. tl;dr: we need an explicit allowlist for *metrics* tags, everything else goes to events/logs only (sampled).

Sean Gallagher: +1. last time we shipped “just one more label” we paged ourselves for 2 days :)

Logan Wright: agreed. i pulled the draft schema list from the shadow event PR and the dashboard mockups. some dims are fine for events, but Prom/OTel metrics need hard constraints.

Nadia Rahman: can we clarify “metrics” vs “event payload”? we’re planning: (a) shadow_pair event (control+shadow) into analytics store, (b) pre-agg metrics emitted from ingestion/scoring.

Allison Grant: yep. this thread is about labels/dimensions on (b) pre-agg metrics (time series). event payload can be richer *if sampled* + retention is short.

Caleb Johnson: i’ll paste the curren...

title: #eng-sre: 1768014202
preview:
Allison Grant: pulling this into #eng-sre for eyes. SDK parity is about to change default retry/backoff behavior (429/5xx + network). Need to sanity check load impact + agree on rollout monitoring + rollback triggers.

Ibrahim Khan: thx. current draft defaults: retry on 429, 408, 409, 500, 502, 503, 504 + common net...
text:
Allison Grant: pulling this into #eng-sre for eyes. SDK parity is about to change default retry/backoff behavior (429/5xx + network). Need to sanity check load impact + agree on rollout monitoring + rollback triggers.

Ibrahim Khan: thx. current draft defaults: retry on 429, 408, 409, 500, 502, 503, 504 + common network errors. respect `Retry-After` (seconds + http-date). backoff = exp w/ jitter. cap attempts. streaming: no automatic retries after first byte.

Sean Gallagher: +1 on the check. retries are where “helpful” turns into thundering herd. what are we actually shipping as defaults? max attempts and initial delay?

Ibrahim Khan: proposal (aligned across py/ts/go): max_attempts=3 total (1 try + 2 retries). base_delay=250ms, max_delay=8s. full jitter (rand(0, backoff)). for 429, if Retry-After present, use it (bounded by max_delay).

Ethan Park: note platform PR is adding Retry-A...

title: #eng-sre: 1767982209
preview:
Sean Gallagher: dropping the first cut of SLO/alert proposal for shadow scoring + shadow target health. want eyes esp on paging policy before we lock for beta.

Sean Gallagher: tl;dr: shadowing is *fail-open* so we shouldn’t page on “shadow is broken” unless it’s (a) causing user-path harm (latency/quota), or (b) we...
text:
Sean Gallagher: dropping the first cut of SLO/alert proposal for shadow scoring + shadow target health. want eyes esp on paging policy before we lock for beta.

Sean Gallagher: tl;dr: shadowing is *fail-open* so we shouldn’t page on “shadow is broken” unless it’s (a) causing user-path harm (latency/quota), or (b) we’re blind during an active rollout where people expect quality signals.

Allison Grant: +1. we need to separate “customer impact” from “product usefulness”. most of these are usefulness signals.

Sean Gallagher: proposed SLIs (per tenant+route+experiment, but alerts aggregate up to avoid cardinality):
1) scoring lag: time from `shadow_pair_ingested_ts` -> `scores_written_ts`
2) scoring availability: % of shadow pairs that eventually get a score within window
3) shadow target error rate: % mirror attempts that return non-2xx (or transport errors)
4) mirror overhead: added CP...

title: #eng-sre: 1764542210
preview:
Sean Gallagher: We need to put an SLO around *gate execution latency* for Optimize quality gates. Right now rollout controller is basically “wait for gates”, and when eval-harness queues back up we end up blocking canary pipelines for 30–60+ min. That’s not acceptable as we go wider in Feb.

Sean Gallagher: Proposal...
text:
Sean Gallagher: We need to put an SLO around *gate execution latency* for Optimize quality gates. Right now rollout controller is basically “wait for gates”, and when eval-harness queues back up we end up blocking canary pipelines for 30–60+ min. That’s not acceptable as we go wider in Feb.

Sean Gallagher: Proposal: define an SLI “time from gate requested -> terminal state (passed/failed/overridden/timeout)” and set tiered SLOs. If we can’t meet, it should fail with a clear reason (SLO exceeded) and require explicit override (no silent pass).

Allison Grant: +1 on defining the SLI precisely. We should split it into at least two measurements though:
1) queue latency (time waiting for GPU / worker) and
2) execution latency (harness runtime)
Otherwise we won’t know if it’s a capacity issue vs a perf regression.

Sergio Costa: What numbers are we thinking? We’ll need thresholds per tier ...
